A report by Machinery Market detailed Talisman Plastics’ implementation of a Keyence hybrid laser marker to improve production efficiency and mark durability across high-volume plastic components.

Talisman Plastics invested in a new Keyence hybrid laser marker to upgrade its part marking operations, leveraging the marker’s hybrid laser technology to achieve faster marking speeds, permanent marks on plastics, and lower consumable costs.

Keyence announced it would report its second quarter FY2021 consolidated financial results on October 30, 2020, covering net sales, operating income, and net profit, and that management would hold a webcast to discuss the quarter’s performance and outlook.

Keyence began shipments of the XM Series handheld probe 3D coordinate measuring machine, featuring a lightweight, portable design and high-speed scanning capabilities that enable users to perform precise, on-site dimensional inspections of complex parts without the need for fixtures.

Keyence unveiled the MD‑X Series Hybrid Laser Marker, which combines fiber and CO₂ laser technologies in a single compact unit to provide high-speed, high-contrast marking on a wide variety of materials with minimal maintenance requirements.

Keyence released its Q2 FY2021 earnings, reporting year-over-year increases in net sales and operating income driven by strong demand in factory automation sensors and measurement systems, and announced an interim dividend reflecting continued profitability.

Submitted Q2 FY2021 consolidated financial report with sales of JPY238,755 million (+13.8% YoY), operating profit JPY116,772 million (+15.9% YoY), ordinary profit JPY120,245 million (+13.7% YoY), and net profit JPY84,323 million (+14.5% YoY)

TMC Announces Changes to Executive Structure, Senior Professional/Senior Management Employees, and Organizational Structure. Toyota announced changes to its executive and organizational structures, effective January 1, 2021, to respond to external environment changes and strengthen management flexibility.

Toyota Launches the New Mirai. Toyota launched the completely redesigned Mirai fuel cell electric vehicle (FCEV), emphasizing premium styling, expanded cruising range, and advanced hydrogen-based technology.

Toyota Moves Closer to Production with Next Generation Fuel Cell Electric Technology for Zero-Emissions Heavy Duty Trucks. Toyota unveiled production-intent prototype Class 8 heavy-duty fuel cell electric trucks, showcasing a new compact hydrogen storage and powertrain system with over 300 miles of range.

Toyota Motor North America Announces Executive Changes. TMNA announced several executive appointments and promotions, effective January 4, 2021, to strengthen its production engineering, marketing, and R&D leadership in North America.

TMC Decides to Repurchase and Cancel First Series Model AA Class Shares. Toyota decided to repurchase and cancel all outstanding First Series Model AA Class Shares, reinforcing mid‑to‑long‑term funding for cutting‑edge R&D initiatives.

Every Step Counts – Toyota Releases its 2020 North American Environmental Report. Toyota released its 2020 North American environmental report, highlighting a 9% reduction in GHG emissions, expanded electrified product launches, and water stewardship efforts.

Safety Recall 20TA02 Amended to Add Approximately 8,000 Vehicles. Toyota amended its fuel pump safety recall to include approximately 8,000 additional vehicles equipped with low‑pressure fuel pumps that may stall, potentially increasing crash risk.

Toyota Launches "C+pod" Ultra-Compact Battery Electric Vehicle in Japan. Toyota launched the "C+pod" BEV, a two-seater ultra‑compact vehicle designed for corporate and urban mobility, with limited launch to select partners.
COVID-19 cases at Toyota facilities (disclosed Mar–Dec 2020). Toyota disclosed multiple COVID-19 cases among employees at various facilities in Japan, confirming no impact on production operations.

Toyota GAZOO Racing outlined its 2020 Yaris WRC lineup for Rallye Monte-Carlo, featuring drivers Ogier, Evans, and Rovanperä.
Toyota Indiana completed a $1.3 billion modernization project, adding 550 new jobs and aligning production with common architectures for North America.
Toyota provided aid for COVID‑19 response measures in China, offering medical vehicles and donations to support healthcare professionals.
Toyota Motor Corp's production plants in China will remain closed through Feb. 9 in line with local transport lockdowns due to the coronavirus outbreak.
Toyota Motor Corp raised its annual operating profit forecast by 4.2% to ¥2.5 trillion for the year to March 2020, citing a slightly weaker yen.
Toyota GAZOO Racing outlined its 2020 global motorsports activities—including rally, endurance, and touring car programs—positioning racing as a foundation for vehicle development.
Toyota Motor Corp said production at all its China plants would remain suspended through Feb. 16, extending the shutdown amid coronavirus supply chain disruptions.
Toyota Financial Services issued a $750 million unsecured Green Bond in Plano, Texas, reinforcing its commitment to environmentally sustainable financing.
Toyota launched all-wheel drive variants of the Camry and Avalon in the U.S., featuring the first AWD Camry since 1991 and first-ever AWD Avalon with Dynamic Torque Control AWD.
Toyota Motor Corp suspended production at its St. Petersburg plant from March 6 and halted vehicle imports into Russia indefinitely due to supply chain disruptions.
Toyota confirmed a second COVID‑19 infection at its Takaoka Plant in Toyota City, closing the affected line for three days to disinfect and isolating 33 team members as a precaution.
Toyota announced halting operations on seven production lines across five Japanese plants from April 3 to address declining demand amid the pandemic.
Toyota and Nippon Telegraph and Telephone (NTT) entered into a business and capital memorandum of understanding to collaborate on next‑generation mobility services.
Toyota extended the suspension of all its European factories (except Russia) until April 20 at earliest due to the spread of COVID‑19.
